latestIssue.subject: ISSUE 60610 · 8 STORIES · 11 MIN READ
latestIssue.intro: The AI infrastructure boom is hitting turbulence from all sides today — Seattle just became the largest U.S. city to ban new AI data centers, Google is slashing subscription prices in what looks like the opening salvo of a commoditization war, and Meta is meanwhile doubling down with a massive new data center deal in India. It's a day that really captures the tension between AI's breakneck expansion and the growing pushback against its real-world costs, with stories ranging from deepfake abuse on X to a surprisingly functional Siri finally showing up to the party.

recentIssues.subject: ISSUE 60610 · 8 STORIES · 11 MIN READ
recentIssues.intro: The AI infrastructure boom is hitting turbulence from all sides today — Seattle just became the largest U.S. city to ban new AI data centers, Google is slashing subscription prices in what looks like the opening salvo of a commoditization war, and Meta is meanwhile doubling down with a massive new data center deal in India. It's a day that really captures the tension between AI's breakneck expansion and the growing pushback against its real-world costs, with stories ranging from deepfake abuse on X to a surprisingly functional Siri finally showing up to the party.

recentIssues.subject: ISSUE 60609 · 8 STORIES · 11 MIN READ
recentIssues.intro: The big story today is Apple finally going all-in on AI — and doing it in a way that's turning heads. From a revamped Siri powered by Google's Gemini to letting users "vibe-code" their own Safari extensions with natural language, WWDC 2026 painted a picture of a company embedding AI deep into its operating system rather than chasing hype, all while taking a notably humbler approach to demos after that $250 million false advertising settlement. Beyond Cupertino, we've also got China's wild new underwater datacenter, a VC valuation scandal brewing on X, and some tough news for Sam Altman's eye-scanning venture — plenty to dig into today.

recentIssues.subject: ISSUE 60608 · 8 STORIES · 11 MIN READ
recentIssues.intro: The AI honeymoon phase might be officially over — at least for your wallet. Microsoft's shift to per-token pricing for GitHub Copilot has sparked what Reddit is calling the "Tokenpocalypse," and it's part of a broader reckoning as companies realize just how expensive AI really is when the subsidies dry up. Today we're also diving into how scammers are weaponizing AI to fake insurance claims at record scale, OpenAI's ambitious "super app" pivot, and a growing debate about what we lose when we let AI do our thinking for us.

recentIssues.subject: ISSUE 60607 · 8 STORIES · 11 MIN READ
recentIssues.intro: The lines between AI and government are blurring fast — the Trump administration is reportedly in talks to take an equity stake in OpenAI, a move that could fundamentally reshape the relationship between Washington and Silicon Valley. But that's far from the only big story today: Apple is gearing up to unveil a Gemini-powered Siri overhaul at WWDC, OpenAI is rolling out a new "Lockdown Mode" to combat prompt injection attacks, and a troubling investigation reveals how ChatGPT is unwittingly steering shoppers toward sophisticated scam websites. It's a day that underscores just how quickly AI is weaving itself into everything from national policy to your shopping cart — and not always in ways we'd hope.

recentIssues.subject: ISSUE 60606 · 8 STORIES · 11 MIN READ
recentIssues.intro: The biggest story today is one that reads like science fiction but is very real: Google is shelling out a staggering $920 million *per month* to SpaceX for GPU access, hot on the heels of Anthropic's even larger $1.25 billion monthly compute deal — underscoring just how ferociously the AI giants are scrambling for infrastructure as demand outpaces supply. Beyond the compute arms race, today's digest spans everything from a Bluetooth speaker that can silently hijack your PC, to an AI-faked presidential speech that went viral across Africa because it said what real leaders won't, to a growing wave of startups that — ironically — want to pull you away from your screen entirely.

recentIssues.subject: ISSUE 60605 · 8 STORIES · 11 MIN READ
recentIssues.intro: Anthropic's confidential IPO filing is arguably the biggest story in AI today — with annualized revenue soaring to $47 billion and a $965 billion valuation, the company is making a massive bet that the AI boom is far from over. But that's just one thread in a day packed with big moves: Mira Murati resurfaced with a tantalizing preview of "interaction models" that could reshape how we communicate with AI, Airbnb's Brian Chesky is launching his own AI lab out of frustration with existing models, and Canada unveiled a national AI strategy aimed at reducing dependence on the U.S. Buckle in — there's a lot to unpack.

recentIssues.subject: ISSUE 60604 · 8 STORIES · 11 MIN READ
recentIssues.intro: SpaceX is gearing up for what could be the largest IPO in history, potentially pushing Elon Musk into trillionaire territory — and that's just one of several massive stories today. We've also got Seattle delivering a striking rebuke to Big Tech with a datacenter moratorium, a vibe-coding startup crossing $400 million in revenue with barely 150 employees, and a tech journalist's surprisingly emotional year living as an AI lab rat. Let's dive in.

recentIssues.subject: ISSUE 60603 · 8 STORIES · 11 MIN READ
recentIssues.intro: The AI revolution is pushing into every corner of our world today — from a $2,000 feature film heading to Tribeca that's forcing us to rethink what "real" filmmaking looks like, to researchers discovering how AI can supercharge computer worms into unprecedented cyber threats. Meanwhile, the business side is just as dramatic, with South Korea's chip giants riding the AI boom into the trillion-dollar club even as bubble fears mount, and data security startup Cyera eyeing a jaw-dropping $12 billion valuation at 80x revenue. Buckle in — today's digest covers the creative, the dangerous, and the financially dizzying sides of AI's accelerating impact.

recentIssues.subject: ISSUE 60602 · 8 STORIES · 11 MIN READ
recentIssues.intro: The big money is flowing — and so are the big concerns. Alphabet's staggering $80 billion fundraise to fuel its AI buildout dominates today's headlines, underscoring just how aggressively tech giants are betting on AI infrastructure, with collective industry capex expected to hit $700 billion this year. But it's not all bullish momentum: from Florida suing OpenAI over child safety risks, to hackers exploiting Meta's AI support bot to hijack Obama's Instagram account, to Nvidia making its play for the $200 billion PC market with AI agent-ready chips, today's digest is a vivid reminder that the AI gold rush is raising as many red flags as it is dollar signs.

recentIssues.subject: ISSUE 60601 · 8 STORIES · 11 MIN READ
recentIssues.intro: The AI backlash is getting louder — and more creative. From college grads booing AI mentions and DuckDuckGo downloads surging 30% as users flee Google's AI push, to Erin Brockovich crowdsourcing a map of secretive data centers and UK charities fighting AI-powered age checks on child asylum seekers, today's stories paint a picture of a society grappling with where to draw the line. We've also got tech billionaires dreaming of conscious AI conquering the cosmos, a surprisingly thoughtful book on knowledge graphs, and yes, an AI weed vape that pays you in Bitcoin — because of course that exists.

recentIssues.subject: ISSUE 60531 · 8 STORIES · 11 MIN READ
recentIssues.intro: The developer world is buzzing today as GitHub Copilot's surprise switch to token-based billing has some users staring down projected monthly costs jumping from $50 to over $3,000 — and they're not happy about it. But pricing drama isn't the only big move shaking things up: SoftBank is pouring a staggering €75 billion into French data centers, Meta's cooking up an AI pendant, and Google's new always-on assistant Gemini Spark is getting its first real-world test drive. From your wallet to your wardrobe to the Vatican, AI is making itself at home everywhere — let's dig in.

recentIssues.subject: ISSUE 60530 · 8 STORIES · 11 MIN READ
recentIssues.intro: The AI productivity paradox is getting harder to ignore. Developers are now refusing to work without AI coding tools, yet mounting evidence suggests AI-generated code creates 1.7x more problems — and companies are spending nearly half their AI tokens just fixing bugs that AI itself introduced. Pair that with today's stories on "AI psychosis" driving reckless workforce cuts, a massive 17-million-device botnet takedown, and a startup offering free house cleaning just to film training data for robots, and it's a day that really puts the gap between AI hype and AI reality under the microscope.
